key_strengths: Complete monopoly on EUV lithography — sole supplier of machines required to manufacture every advanced chip on earth
27% ROIC, 51–53% gross margins, and €11B in free cash flow sustained across semiconductor cycles
€38.8B order backlog (~1.2x annual revenue) with EUV capacity fully booked through 2027
Fortress balance sheet: net cash ~€10B+, Debt/EBITDA below 0.3x, A1 credit rating
30-year technology lead embedded in physics, supplier ecosystems, and customer process dependencies — no credible rival before 2028–2030
key_risks: China DUV export ban risk: China was ~25–30% of 2025 revenue; a blanket ban would remove ~€8B annually with no near-term substitute
Valuation compression: stock trades at ~48–52x trailing earnings, 40–45% above 10-year historical average P/E of 36x
Semiconductor capex cycle downturn: correlated cuts by TSMC, Samsung, and Intel would compress order intake sharply beyond the 12–18 month backlog buffer
High-NA EUV ramp execution risk: margin expansion thesis depends on adoption scaling to 20+ units/year by 2027; any delay would hit revenue and margins
Geopolitical tail risk: TSMC (~33% of EUV demand) exposure to Taiwan Strait disruption; China rare-earth supply controls add supply-side vulnerability
red_flags: Customer concentration extreme: ~60–75% of revenue from three customers (TSMC, Samsung, Intel); TSMC capex pause would be immediately painful
China revenue structurally declining due to export restrictions; must be replaced by growth elsewhere
Stock rose ~50% in 2025; momentum-driven positioning increases risk of sharp sentiment-driven reversals
High correlation with AI/semiconductor theme adds portfolio concentration risk rather than diversification
China EUV prototype development reported December 2025 — earlier-stage than feared but warrants monitoring through 2026–2028
buy_triggers: Price declines 30–40% to approximately $950–1,050 (NASDAQ), bringing forward P/E to ~28–32x and creating a plausible base-case return of 8–12% annually
Semiconductor capex downturn creates a sentiment-driven drawdown with no change to ASML's monopoly position or long-term demand trajectory
China DUV risk formally clarified and priced in (e.g., blanket ban announced, stock re-rates to €700–800/share, remaining ~75% of revenue revalued on its own merits)
sell_triggers: Blanket DUV export ban materializes AND TSMC or Samsung simultaneously reduces EUV capex guidance by more than 20% — breaking the thesis from both sides
ASML moat shows credible deterioration: Chinese EUV prototype achieves yields above 50% on sub-10nm nodes, or High-NA EUV adoption stalls with shipment guidance cut below 20 units/year by 2027
Stock trades above 60x forward earnings while ASML's 2030 revenue trajectory is tracking the bull case — multiple pricing near-perfection on already-optimistic execution
valuation_summary: Conservative intrinsic value estimates land at €500–800/share versus a current price of ~€1,260/share (~$1,368 NASDAQ); the base case barely justifies today's price and only the bull case produces meaningful upside, with bear-case downside of 55–60% and no margin of safety at current levels.
source_summary: Analysis based on web search results (January–March 2026) including ASML Q4 2025 press release, ASML investor relations, MacroTrends, StockAnalysis, Morningstar, GuruFocus, analyst price targets from MarketBeat/TipRanks, and China export control news coverage; training knowledge used for technical and governance context.
